name: UploadCheck Pipeline Recipes
description: Machine-readable defaults for agentic production pipelines that call UploadCheck before upload.

launch_preflight.rule: Before launch-sensitive production workflows, verify product_hunt_ready=true and remaining_blockers is empty. If blockers remain, report them before running publish or Product Hunt launch steps.

cost_preflight.rule: Run cost preflight before uploading large media or requesting model-backed checks.

repair_loop: Fetch qc_get_report and qc_get_marker_csv after the job completes.
Group flags by BLOCK, WATCH, and PASS.
Show all flags to the user and ask whether to fix now.
Apply only fixes reachable from source files, captions, metadata, or manifests.
For render defects, return timestamped source/render instructions and rerun UploadCheck after the media changes.

repair_loop_contract.user_prompt: Show all QC flags and ask: Fix now?
repair_loop_contract.source_or_render_instruction: Return timestamped source/render patch instructions instead of pretending the agent can edit unreachable media.
repair_loop_contract.completion_rule: Do not call the asset upload-ready until UploadCheck is rerun after edits and no BLOCK flags remain.

nto_replacement_qc.purpose: Expose the NTO/NPO production-pipeline QC categories UploadCheck can replace without publishing private thresholds or implementation details.
nto_replacement_qc.private_moat_rule: Publish defect categories, user-facing outcomes, and repair-loop behavior; keep exact thresholds, prompts, fixtures, and validator internals private.

implemented_gates.covers: Poorly contrasting overlay text over image, footage, or background.
Text hidden by platform UI, cropped near the edge, or unsafe for Shorts action chrome.
Long-form pillarbox, letterbox, portrait-in-landscape, and black-gutter layout defects.
9:16 export shape, full-bleed framing, duration, opening/footer text-card presence, and no-dialogue Shorts constraints.
Exact visual reuse, source-family dominance, and cheap repeated visual patterns from storybook/edit manifests.
A named speaker's voice paired with another character's face in a storybook/edit manifest.
Held talking-head or portrait shots that dominate a long-form window without motion, graphic, source-card, or b-roll relief.
Narration names a person, place, date, source, or event, but the visual shows only generic mood footage instead of the named subject or an explicit source-card/Remotion fallback.
Generic openings, missing hook frame/card, and title/thumbnail/opening-frame mismatch when an opening manifest is supplied.
Missing next-video tease, CTA, footer card, or episode handoff in the final window when a manifest is supplied.
Long-form stretches that go too long without a pattern interrupt, retention re-hook, chapter turn, source card, or visual reset.
Before/after visual proof for repaired complaint windows and regression-prone visual fixes.
Unintended long silence or audio dropouts.
Spoken stage directions, vendor names, URLs, prompt text, markdown, and production notes.
Narration drift from locked script when transcript and expected script are supplied.
Voice-clip shorts or excerpts ending mid-word, mid-sentence, or before the punchline resolves when transcript sidecars are supplied.
Customer/project-specific names, banned misrenders, and terms that must be spoken correctly.
Duplicate AI characters, clone crowds, same-face extras, and scenes that need more character variation.
Degraded, black-and-white, silent-era, low-resolution, or generic filler footage unless intentionally archival.
Visual/narration mismatch where footage does not illustrate the spoken beat.

recommended_sidecars.manifest_path: Storybook, edit-decision, or visual-timeline JSON for repeat_fatigue, speaker_visual_binding, static_head_dominance, literal_subject_match, first_three_seconds, end_screen_tease, rehook_cadence, contact_sheet_evidence, and source-family dominance.
recommended_sidecars.transcript_path: Final transcript text or JSON for spoken_leaks, pronunciation_watchlist, and script_faithfulness.
recommended_sidecars.watchlist_path: Customer/project terms with expected names and banned misrenders.

recommended_sidecars.manifest_path: Shorts edit manifest or clip list for repeated visual reuse, 0-3s hook-card verification, and final footer-card verification.
recommended_sidecars.transcript_path: Short transcript or caption text for spoken_leaks and Shorts text-card checks.
Final transcript for deterministic leak, pronunciation, and script-drift checks.
recommended_sidecars.watchlist_path: Names, sponsor terms, organization names, and banned misrenders.
recommended_sidecars.expected_script_path: Locked script or rundown when available.
